# sixtom

> AI ships demos. I ship products. I run AI agents in parallel — work happens whether I'm in a meeting or asleep. Two weeks to a working version. $500 audit. $7,500 sprint. 1 client a month.

## What I do

- **The Audit — $500.** You send me your repo and the thing you're stuck on. I send back a 1-pager and a 15-min Loom — what's blocking, what I'd do, whether a sprint makes sense. About a week turnaround.
- **The Async Sprint — $7,500.** Two weeks. Multiple agents working in parallel on the boring bits — fixes, accessibility passes, the edge cases that break in front of customers. I steer. You get a daily drop in Slack and a working version live on day 10.

## Who it's for

- A prototype that won't ship.
- An internal tool that won't scale past the person who built it.
- A team that built with AI but stalls before production.

## How the sprint goes

- Week 1 day 0: 30-min call (or skip if you did the audit). We figure out the thing.
- Days 1–7: Agents build. I steer. Daily Loom + code drop in Slack.
- Mid-sprint: 1 short sync. Course-correct if needed.
- Day 5: If we can both see it won't ship in scope, we stop. You keep what we built.
- Day 10: Live in production. You own it.
- Day 30: Check-in.

## How I work

I orchestrate AI agents continuously — drafts, fixes, accessibility passes, the parts that don't need taste. I bring the taste. The fleet runs in parallel, which is why a two-week sprint can produce a shipping-ready product. You don't have to be on a call; the work happens whether you are or not.

## Pricing

- Audit: $500 USD, fixed. Turnaround within a week.
- Sprint: $7,500 USD, fixed. 1 client a month, by appointment.
- If by sprint day 5 we can both see it won't ship in scope, we stop. You keep what we built. Pay for the time, that's it.

## How to engage

- Book a 30-min intro call (link on the homepage).
- Notify list for the next open slot (form on the homepage).
